Class Firefox

java.lang.Object
uno.anahata.asi.agi.tool.ToolContext
uno.anahata.asi.agi.tool.AnahataToolkit
uno.anahata.asi.yam.tools.browser.AbstractBrowser
uno.anahata.asi.yam.tools.firefox.Firefox
All Implemented Interfaces:
ContextProvider, PropertyChangeSource, Rebindable
public class Firefox extends AbstractBrowser
A toolkit for web automation and form filling using Firefox and Selenium.

This toolkit manages a fleet of BrowserDrone instances utilizing the GeckoDriver for Firefox automation.

Author:
anahata

  • Constructor Details

    • Firefox

      public Firefox()

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public void initialize()
      Performs initial setup for a newly created toolkit instance.

      This method is called exactly once by the ToolManager after the toolkit has been instantiated and registered. It provides a clean hook for toolkits to perform one-time initialization logic that requires the tool environment to be fully configured, such as scanning for project instructions or registering default resources.

      Initializes the toolkit, disabling it by default if it is in beta.

      Overrides:
      initialize in class AnahataToolkit

    • rebind

      public void rebind()
      Rebinds the toolkit after deserialization.

      This method is called by the framework during session activation to restore transient state, reconnect listeners, or refresh cached project instances. It ensures the propertyChangeSupport is re-initialized.

      Rebinds the transient driver states after session deserialization.

      Specified by:
      rebind in interface Rebindable
      Overrides:
      rebind in class AnahataToolkit

    • postActivate

      public void postActivate()
      Callback method triggered by the Asi Container once the Agi session has been fully deserialized and associated to the ASI container.

      Clears orphaned drones after session deserialization, as Firefox cannot reliably reconnect.

      Overrides:
      postActivate in class AnahataToolkit

    • getSystemInstructions

      public List<String> getSystemInstructions()
      Gets a list of system instruction strings provided by this context provider. These are typically prepended to the conversation as high-level guidance.

      Provides context-specific instructions to the ASI regarding Firefox automation.

      Returns:
      A list of system instruction strings.

    • populateMessage

      public void populateMessage(RagMessage ragMessage)
      Populates the given RagMessage with dynamic, just-in-time context parts. These parts are appended to the end of the user's prompt (RAG).

      Populates the RAG message with current Firefox environment state.

      Parameters:
      ragMessage - The message to be augmented with context.

    • connect

      public String connect(String droneId, String profile, Boolean headless, String dataDir, String binaryPath) throws AgiToolException
      Configures FirefoxOptions with headless and profile flags. Launches the process via the system's default GeckoDriverService.
      Parameters:
      droneId - The unique ID for the drone.
      profile - The Firefox profile name.
      headless - Whether to run without a GUI.
      dataDir - The user data directory.
      binaryPath - The path to the Firefox binary.
      Returns:
      A status message confirming the launch.
      Throws:
      AgiToolException - If the drone ID is already in use.

    • killAll

      public String killAll()
      Closes all active browser processes.
      Specified by:
      killAll in class AbstractBrowser
      Returns:
      A status message describing the cleanup operations.

    • listTabs

      public List<String> listTabs(String droneId)
      Lists all open tabs/windows in the specified drone.

      Implementations should ideally use native DevTools protocols to avoid forcing window focus and causing screen flickering.

      Specified by:
      listTabs in class AbstractBrowser
      Parameters:
      droneId - The ID of the drone.
      Returns:
      A list of formatted tab titles and URLs.

    • getDriver

      public org.openqa.selenium.WebDriver getDriver(String droneId)
      Resolves the active WebDriver for the requested drone.

      Implementation details: This is an internal lifecycle method. If the connection is lost, concrete implementations should attempt to re-establish it using the cached port and profile data.

      Specified by:
      getDriver in class AbstractBrowser
      Parameters:
      droneId - The unique ID of the drone.
      Returns:
      The active WebDriver, or null if it cannot be resolved.

    • launchFirefoxInternal

      private String launchFirefoxInternal(BrowserDrone d)
      Executes the native Firefox launch sequence.

      Implementation details: Uses CompletableFuture to launch the driver in a background thread to prevent blocking the tool execution flow. Sets a 60-second timeout for the initial handshake.

      Parameters:
      d - The drone to launch.
      Returns:
      A success or error message.
